items.title: Update Hatchling Dependency for Enhanced Compatibility in Suno Cli 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the hatchling requirement in the pyproject.toml file to allow for versions between 1.21.0 and 1.30.0. This change ensures compatibility with the latest features and fixes introduced in hatchling v1.29.0, enhancing the overall functionality of the Suno Cli project.

items.title: Upgrade k8s-set-context to v4 for enhanced compatibility in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the Azure/k8s-set-context dependency from version 1 to version 4, ensuring compatibility with Node 20 as Node 16 is deprecated. The change impacts the deployment workflow by updating the action used to set the Kubernetes context, enhancing overall stability and performance.

items.title: Upgrade Checkout Action to Version 6 in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the actions/checkout from version 4 to version 6, enhancing compatibility with Node.js 24 and improving credential management by persisting them to a separate file. The changes also include updates to the CI workflows to reflect the new version, ensuring smoother operations and better performance.

items.title: Upgrade Docker Login Action to Version 4 in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the Docker Login Action from version 2 to version 4, enhancing the authentication process for Docker registries. Key changes include switching to Node 24 as the default runtime and updating various dependencies to improve performance and security. This upgrade ensures compatibility with the latest features and optimizations.

items.title: Upgrade Django to 4.2.29 in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps Django from version 4.2.2 to 4.2.29, incorporating several important security fixes and improvements. Key changes include resolutions for multiple CVEs, enhancing the framework's security and stability. The modifications are reflected in both the poetry.lock and pyproject.toml files, ensuring compatibility with the latest features.

items.title: Streamline API Navigation by Removing Extra Dropdowns in Docs 🚀
items.summary: This update simplifies the API Reference section by eliminating the unnecessary 'API Reference' dropdown layer across all service groups. By removing the top-level 'tags' array from 34 OpenAPI spec files, endpoints are now directly accessible under their respective service groups, enhancing user navigation and experience.

items.title: Add SKALE Base payment option in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces SKALE Base as a new payment network option in the X402Pay component, allowing users to transact with zero gas fees. Key changes include the addition of SKALE chain ID mapping, updated network labels, and necessary parameters for wallet integration. Users selecting SKALE will have their wallets automatically switched to the SKALE Base network for seamless transactions.

items.title: Exciting SKALE Base Chain Support Added in x402 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the SkaleChainHandler for the SKALE Base mainnet, enabling zero gas fees for transactions on this EVM-compatible chain. Key changes include new environment configurations, integration of SKALE-specific settlement handling, and the addition of the SKALE asset for bridging. The implementation enhances asset transfer capabilities while maintaining EIP-712 verification logic.

items.title: Publishers Now Live! 🚀 in Publisher
items.summary: This update fixes the publishing functionality for several API publishers, including Zhihu, CSDN, JueJin, CNBlogs, Dev.to, and Medium. Previously, these platforms only created drafts, but now they successfully publish articles live. Key changes include updates to the publishing logic and status settings across multiple files, ensuring a seamless publishing experience.

items.title: Exciting New SKALE Payment Option Added in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: We've introduced SKALE Base as a new payment network option in the X402Pay component, allowing for zero gas fees. Key updates include the addition of SKALE chain ID mapping, a new label for SKALE in the network formatting, and updated chain parameters for wallet integration. This enhancement aims to streamline payment processes for users on the SKALE network.

items.title: Add SKALE Base as a new X402 payment option in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces SKALE Base as a third payment option for X402, alongside Base and Solana, allowing for zero gas fees. Key changes include the addition of new environment variables in `core/settings.py` and modifications to `app/utils/x402_payment.py` to handle SKALE network transactions. This enhancement aims to improve payment flexibility and reduce costs for users.

items.title: Enhance Sora Workers with String Version Checks in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies all four Sora workers (ttapi, openaihk, mountsea, cqtai) to use string coercion for version checks, ensuring compatibility with version '2.0'. The changes enhance error handling by throwing a BadRequestError for unsupported versions. This update is a companion to the recent changes in the PlatformBackend.

items.title: Updated Version Parameter to String Format in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update changes the version parameter from an integer to a string format, specifically '1.0' and '2.0'. Key files modified include API definitions, documentation, and test cases to reflect this change. This adjustment enhances consistency and clarity across the platform's backend services.

items.title: Enhance Explore Page Descriptions in Dify 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the missing workflow descriptions on the Explore page by ensuring that the App's description is synced with the Site record. The changes include modifications to the import logic, allowing for the update of Site descriptions when they are absent. This improvement enhances the user experience by providing clearer information on the Explore page.

items.title: Revamp API Publishing for 25 Platforms in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces Playwright browser automation with pure HTTP API calls for 25 out of 27 publishing platforms, significantly improving publish times from ~30-60 seconds to just 1-3 seconds. It also ports 18 platform adapters from TypeScript to Python and implements 5 new publishers using official APIs. A comprehensive test suite with 141 tests has been added to ensure reliability and performance.

items.title: Normalize Version and Duration in Sora Handlers in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update normalizes the version and duration fields to numbers across all Sora video handlers. It also implements a rejection for version 2 in non-ttapi workers and adds a Kong canary rule for string version matching. These changes enhance validation and error handling for video requests.

items.title: Streamline API Reference Navigation in Docs 🚀
items.summary: This update simplifies the API reference sidebar by removing the intermediate 'API Reference' section, allowing users to access endpoints directly under each service group. The configuration has been updated from an auto-generated format to an explicit pages format, enhancing clarity and usability across all 15 language configurations.

items.title: Fix prompt and lyric limits in M C P Suno 🎶
items.summary: The suno_list_models tool has been updated to accurately reflect the prompt and lyric limits. A new Lyric Limit column has been added, while the Prompt Limit has been corrected to 200 characters. This change enhances clarity for users regarding model capabilities.

items.title: Update prompt and lyric limits for clarity in Suno API 🎶
items.summary: This update clarifies the distinction between the 'prompt' field and the actual lyrics in the model version table. A new 'lyric Limit' column has been added, accurately reflecting the character limits of 3000-5000 for lyrics. These changes enhance the documentation and improve user understanding of the model capabilities.

items.title: Update Suno Prompt and Lyric Limits in Docs 🎶
items.summary: This update corrects the Suno prompt and lyric limit table across all languages. A new 'lyric Limit' column has been added with accurate values of 3000-5000 characters, while the 'prompt Limit' column now correctly reflects a limit of 200 characters. All 18 language variants have been updated to ensure consistency and clarity.

items.title: Reorganize Workflows for Docker Visibility in Dify 🚀
items.summary: This update moves the `workflows/` directory into `api/` to ensure that the Docker image includes all necessary workflow files. The `WORKFLOWS_DIR` path has been updated accordingly to reflect this change. This fix addresses the issue where no AceDataCloud workflows were found on the Explore page, enhancing the functionality of the application.

items.title: Enhance error handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the error handling for upstream 500, 502, and 504 errors by preventing the exposure of raw error messages to customers. Instead, users will receive a more user-friendly message indicating that the service is temporarily unavailable. This change enhances the overall user experience and maintains security by not leaking sensitive information.

items.title: Expanded Language Support with Greek, Finnish, and Serbian in Docs 🎉
items.summary: This update adds Greek, Finnish, and Serbian language navigation to the documentation by modifying the `docs.json` file. Each language now has complete translated content and is fully integrated, bringing the total to 18 supported languages. All paths and translations have been verified for accuracy and functionality.

items.title: Optimize memory usage in Platform Service 🧠
items.summary: This update addresses a significant memory leak in the nanobanana image processing functionality. Key changes include optimizing logging to prevent large base64 data from being held in memory, and implementing a sequential processing loop to manage candidate uploads more efficiently. As a result, peak memory usage per request has been reduced from approximately 80-120MB to around 15-25MB.

items.title: Boost Node.js Memory for Nanobanana Stability in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses frequent restarts of Nanobanana pods caused by JavaScript heap exhaustion. Key changes include increasing the Node.js heap size to 800MB and raising the memory limits for the deployment from 600Mi to 1024Mi. These adjustments aim to enhance stability and reduce 502 Bad Gateway errors due to pod failures.

items.title: Clean Up Navigation Tabs for a Smoother Experience in Docs ✨
items.summary: This update removes 60 dead navigation tab entries from `docs.json`, addressing 404 errors across all 15 languages. The changes ensure that the navigation now accurately reflects the available content, enhancing user experience and consistency. The tabs for tutorials, comparisons, use cases, and blogs have been eliminated, streamlining the navigation.

items.title: Clean Up Orphaned Content in Docs 🗑️
items.summary: This update removes all orphan content files related to tutorials, comparisons, use cases, and blog posts that were previously removed from the navigation. A total of 2084 files across multiple languages have been deleted, including the associated scripts for generating SEO pages. This cleanup enhances the documentation's clarity and relevance.

items.title: Enhance AceDataCloud Workflow Visibility in Dify 🌟
items.summary: This update improves the visibility of AceDataCloud workflows on the Explore page by implementing a new periodic sync task that runs every 30 minutes, ensuring workflows are registered regardless of user login. Additionally, a hybrid retrieval mode has been introduced, merging local AceDataCloud templates with Dify's official templates while maintaining clear source identification. Users will now see AceDataCloud apps prominently displayed with a distinct badge, enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Streamlined SEO Content by Removing Duplicates in Docs 📚
items.summary: This update removes 26 outdated SEO pages from the comparisons, blog, and use-cases sections, which were superseded by newer canonical versions. Additionally, translations of these deleted pages across 17 languages have been removed, resulting in a total of 42 files deleted and 7,469 lines removed. The navigation in docs.json has been updated for consistency across all languages.

items.title: New Nexior Deployment Tutorials Added in Backend 🚀
items.summary: We've introduced four new deployment tutorials for Nexior, enhancing our documentation for Docker, Netlify, Zeabur, and Tencent Cloud. These guides provide step-by-step instructions tailored for Chinese users, ensuring easy deployment across various platforms. This update complements the existing Vercel tutorial and aims to streamline the deployment process for users in the Asia-Pacific region.

items.title: Enhanced Chat Completions API Schemas in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns all six chat completion API specifications with the official OpenAI API, adding crucial content part types and new request and response fields. Key enhancements include the addition of `input_audio`, `file`, and `tool_calls`, improving functionality and compliance across APIs. The changes have been validated and synced to the production database.

items.title: Streamline API Reference Navigation in Docs 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the intermediate 'Overview' layer from the API Reference tab, allowing direct access to API groups like Gemini AI and Claude AI. Additionally, the introduction pages across 18 languages have been deleted, and relevant scripts have been updated to reflect these changes. Card links on introduction and quickstart pages now correctly point to the authentication section.

items.title: Revamped Sora V2 Pricing and Size-Based Billing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns Sora V2 cost rules with upstream partner pricing, adjusting credits per second for various models. New size-based billing rules have been introduced for 720p and 1024p resolutions, while V1 pricing remains unchanged. Additionally, seven new test cases have been added to ensure robust coverage for the new billing scenarios.

items.title: Enhance OAuth Security with HMAC in Dify 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the session-based OAuth state verification with HMAC-signed state tokens for the AceDataCloud provider. This change resolves issues with 'Invalid or expired OAuth state' errors on mobile browsers by embedding the nonce and timestamp directly in the state URL parameter. The GitHub and Google OAuth flows remain unchanged, ensuring continued functionality across those providers.
